project_overview.project_name: Second Ethiopia Urban Water Supply and Sanitation Project
project_overview.project_development_objective: To increase access to enhanced water supply and sanitation services in an operationally efficient manner in Addis Ababa and selected secondary cities
project_overview.total_financing: US$505 million (IDA Credit US$320 million + IDA Scale Up Facility US$125 million + Government counterpart US$60 million)
project_overview.beneficiaries: 3.38 million people (50% women) - 2.76 million from improved sanitation, 623,000 from improved water supply

geographic_scope.fragile_or_conflict_affected_areas: partial - project implemented during state of emergency declared due to political unrest

key_components.component_name: Sanitation and water supply services improvements in Addis Ababa
key_components.description: Construction of eastern catchment WWTP (80,000 m3/day), sewerage networks, public/communal latrines, operational efficiency improvements, NRW reduction

key_components.component_name: Sanitation and water supply services improvement in secondary cities
key_components.description: Sanitation infrastructure, fecal sludge management, communal/public latrines, vacuum trucks, water supply improvements, operational efficiency in 22 cities
Federal and regional level capacity building, studies, training, equipment

implementation_arrangements.implementing_agency: Ministry of Water, Irrigation and Electricity (MoWIE)
implementation_arrangements.coordination_mechanisms: National WaSH Steering Committee, Federal Project Management Unit, Regional Water Bureaus, Town Support Groups
implementation_arrangements.procurement_approach: World Bank procurement guidelines, Design-Build-Operate contracts for WWTPs, performance-based contracts
implementation_arrangements.monitoring_and_evaluation: Quarterly reporting, midterm review, annual audits, beneficiary surveys

identified_risks.risk_description: State of emergency and political unrest, tariff revision delays due to political factors
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Monitor political situation, support tariff revision processes, alternative financing mechanisms

identified_risks.risk_description: Limited capacity of secondary cities in implementation and management, weak coordination among multiple stakeholders
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Stepped approach, capacity building, Town Support Groups, international technical assistance

identified_risks.risk_description: Limited experience in WWTP design and management, low demand for sewer connections
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Design-Build-Operate contracts, community engagement, affordable connection strategies

identified_risks.risk_description: Weak financial management and procurement capacity in secondary cities, audit backlogs
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Capacity building, additional FM staff, procurement training, audit backlog clearance plans

identified_risks.risk_description: Land acquisition requirements, NIMBY syndrome for sanitation facilities, limited awareness
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Resettlement Policy Framework, community consultations, Urban Health Extension Program engagement

safeguards_and_social.grievance_redress_mechanism: yes - grievance committees at various levels in participating cities to log, track and resolve complaints
safeguards_and_social.gender_considerations: Gender Action Plan focusing on women's participation in consultations, employment opportunities, gender-responsive infrastructure design, institutional capacity for gender mainstreaming

fcv_relevant_details.conflict_sensitivity: Project designed with awareness of political tensions - includes community engagement, grievance mechanisms, and stepped implementation approach to build local ownership
fcv_relevant_details.fragility_context: Rapid urbanization straining services, institutional fragmentation, limited government capacity, high poverty rates in urban areas, recent political unrest leading to state of emergency
fcv_relevant_details.security_considerations: State of emergency declared due to political unrest may impede implementation progress, security situation being monitored
fcv_relevant_details.displacement_or_refugees: no specific mention of displacement or refugees, but project targets low-income areas and vulnerable populations
